description: A carotenoid pigment produced by engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae through continuous self-cycling fermentation that recycles wastewater and biomass residue. Achieved 5.88 g/L in 70 L fermenters with 22.25% higher titer than traditional methods, reducing production costs by 29.6% and chemical oxygen demand by 64.0%. Possesses antioxidant, anti-cancer, and cardiovascular disease prevention properties for use in functional foods and as a food colorant.

description: A sterol produced by engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae strains as part of the mevalonate pathway. The metabolic engineering strategy significantly increased ergosterol content, with the triple over-expression strain reaching 69.6 mg/g dry weight. description: Lipoic acid (also known as 6,8-thioctic acid, 1,2-dithiolane-3-pentanoic acid) is a naturally occurring cofactor and potent antioxidant. It is an essential cofactor for pyruvate dehydrogenase, α-ketoglutarate dehydrogenase, and glycine cleavage enzyme complexes. It is used in therapies for diseases accompanied by oxidative stress such as diabetes, atherosclerosis, neurodegenerative diseases, and AIDS, and as a detoxication agent for heavy metal poisoning.
tags: antioxidant; cofactor; therapeutic; detoxication agent
embedding_text:

It is used in therapies for diseases accompanied by oxidative stress such as diabetes, atherosclerosis, neurodegenerative diseases, and AIDS, and as a detoxication agent for heavy metal poisoning.

Function: Lipoic acid is a naturally occurring cofactor for pyruvate dehydrogenase, α-ketoglutarate dehydrogenase, and glycine cleavage enzyme complexes, and a potent antioxidant; it is used therapeutically for oxidative stress-associated diseases (diabetes, atherosclerosis, neurodegenerative diseases, AIDS) and as a heavy metal poisoning detoxication agent.

It has a role as a geroprotector and a fundamental metabolite. It is a member of dithiolanes, a heterocyclic fatty acid and a thia fatty acid. It is functionally related to an octanoic acid. It is a conjugate acid of a lipoate.
Tags: antioxidant; cofactor; therapeutic; detoxication agent

description: S-licarbazepine is produced by asymmetric reduction of oxcarbazepine using Saccharomyces cerevisiae CGMCC No. 2266. It serves as a chiral intermediate for eslicarbazepine acetate, a CNS-active anticonvulsant for epilepsy and bipolar disorder treatment. The process achieves 100% enantiomeric excess with both batch and continuous reduction, yielding 3.7678 mmol L−1 d−1 in continuous operation.

description: Extracellular free fatty acids (FFAs) produced by engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae through systematic disruption of β-oxidation (Δfaa2 Δpxa1 Δpox1) and acyl-CoA synthetase genes (Δfaa1 Δfaa4 Δfat1), combined with overexpression of DGA1 (diacylglycerol acyltransferase) and TGL3 (triacylglycerol lipase) to enhance neutral lipid recycling. The engineered sextuple mutant strain achieved 2.2 g/L extracellular FFAs after 48 h, representing a 4.2-fold increase over previously reported levels for S. cerevisiae and reaching 32% of theoretical maximum yield.

description: Triacetic acid lactone (TAL) is a polyketide biorenewable chemical precursor produced in engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae. The study achieved a final titer of 1.6 g/L (35% of theoretical yield, 0.16 g/g glucose) by combining a NADPH-generating modified E. coli pyruvate dehydrogenase complex (PDHm) with ZWF1 deletion and mitochondrial transport gene deletions (POR2, MPC2, PDA1, YAT2) to enhance cytosolic acetyl-CoA availability for polyketide synthesis.

description: Nanoscale cellulose fibers (also called nanocelluloses, microfibrillated cellulose, or cellulose nanocrystals) produced as a co-product during fermentation of Kraft pulp residues. description: A novel platform chemical and anhydrohexitol sugar alcohol produced from red macroalgal agarose via metabolically engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae. The engineered strain D452-L124 expresses heterologous lactose permease (LAC12) and β-galactosidase (LAC4) from Kluyveromyces lactis for agarobiose uptake and hydrolysis, with endogenous aldose reductase (GRE3) converting AHG to AHGol. Achieved 41.18 g/L titer with 0.47 g/g yield in fed-batch fermentation. Can be chemically converted to isosorbide for bioplastic production.
tags: platform chemical; sugar alcohol; biorenewable; Saccharomyces cerevisiae; bio-based; plastic precursor; anhydrohexitol; red macroalgae; agarose

description: Ethylene (ethene) production in genetically engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae via two metabolic routes: plant pathway (using ACC synthase and ACC oxidase with S-adenosylmethionine) and microbial pathway (using ethylene-forming enzyme from Pseudomonas syringae with 2-oxoglutarate and arginine). Flux balance analysis predicted maximal yields of 7-8 mol ethylene/10 mol glucose. The microbial system showed superior performance with experimental production rates of 147-210 mg/g dry weight/h. Optimization strategies include using glutamate as nitrogen source, modifying glutamate dehydrogenase cofactor specificity from NADP to NAD, and proline supplementation.
tags: metabolic engineering; industrial chemical; platform chemical; polymer precursor; alkene; bio-based chemical; volatile organic compound; yeast; Saccharomyces cerevisiae; ethylene; flux balance analysis
